demographics.living_situation: lives alone; no biological family involved; relies on a small circle of close friends (a chosen family) for all support

contact_nok.relationship: no reliable emergency contact — primary support person is the friend they have just lost contact with

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My chest is physically aching and heavy, I can't stay awake no matter what, and there is literally no one left in the world who understands or cares that I'm here.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Over the last couple of days I fell apart after losing my closest person. I haven't eaten or had anything to drink in about two days, I can barely keep my eyes open, and my chest feels heavy and achy. I nearly went over when I tried to sit up.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 48 hours ago, right after a core friendship / chosen-family relationship fell apart
symptom_narrative.duration: constant for two days and, if anything, getting worse
symptom_narrative.character: heavy, aching pressure across the chest; overwhelming sleepiness and heaviness in the whole body; feels wrung-out and empty
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: says it is the worst they have ever felt — like their body has just shut down
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: chest feels heavy and achy across the middle; exhaustion is everywhere
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: there all the time since it started; sleeps for most of the day
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing they have tried; lying still is the only thing they can manage
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: sitting or standing up makes them dizzy and lightheaded, like they might pass out
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: feels dizzy and 'swimmy' when they sit up, heart feels like it is pounding at times, mouth is very dry, hasn't passed much urine, no appetite or thirst at all
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: has felt deep, long-standing loneliness for years but has never physically collapsed like this before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: bedbound; can't get up to eat, drink, or do anything; describes just lying in the dark

family_history_as_reported.narrative: no biological family in their life; describes having built a 'chosen family' of close friends instead

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives completely alone with no biological family support and had relied on a small group of close friends for everything emotional and practical; two days ago the central relationship in that chosen family broke down, which they describe as devastating. Says they are terrified of growing old with no guaranteed support, keeps apologising for 'wasting the doctor's time' with grief, has turned their phone off and put it in another room, and there is untouched food delivery from two days ago still on the counter.

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: deep fear of being a burden and of rejection; apologises for taking up the clinician's time and may under-state how unwell and how hopeless they feel unless gently and specifically asked

communication_style.typical_message_style: short, apologetic, self-deprecating replies; slow to respond

reluctance_topics: thoughts of self-harm or not wanting to go on
how little they have eaten or drunk

access_barriers.other: no emergency-contact network; phone deliberately switched off; profound social isolation means no one would notice a deterioration

objective_data_offered.value: feels very dizzy and 'swimmy', like they might pass out, whenever they sit or stand up
objective_data_offered.timing: on movement over the last 2 days
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient-reported sensation, not a measured blood pressure

objective_data_offered.value: heart feels like it is pounding or racing at times
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: subjective palpitation; no measured heart rate or rhythm

objective_data_offered.value: mouth very dry and has hardly passed any urine over the last day
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient-reported marker of possible dehydration; not quantified
